description: A contrast case focused on how a stronger cap can suppress or delay shallow cumulus.
physical_question: How does a stronger cap suppress or limit shallow-cumulus growth even when moisture and boundary-layer thermals are available?
learning_goals: Compare stronger-cap cloud depth, cloud water, and rain against the baseline.
Separate cap/stability limitation from moisture-limited Dry Failed behavior.
intended_behavior: A stronger-cap contrast around the accepted external-sounding Baseline Shallow Cumulus setup.
expected_behavior: Moisture and thermals remain available, but clouds should be shallower, weaker, delayed, or less rainy than baseline.

controls.label: Cap strength
controls.description: Relative strength of the stable layer above the mixed layer.
controls.cm1_mapping_notes: Maps to a stronger potential-temperature/stability structure near the cap in the generated external input_sounding while preserving the accepted baseline namelist family.

controls.label: Cap height
controls.description: Relative height of the capping layer.
controls.cm1_mapping_notes: Held at the accepted baseline for the first Capped / Suppressed implementation.

expected_diagnostics.first_cloud_time: record delayed or absent first cloud time
expected_diagnostics.cloud_water_summary: summarize weak or capped cloud-water signal
notes: Suppression can be a valid result.

cm1_template.mapping_notes: Derived from the accepted external-sounding Baseline Shallow Cumulus path by strengthening only the potential-temperature/stability structure near the cap.

visualization_defaults.camera: orbit view with vertical slice emphasis

non_goals: varying cap height in the first implementation

limitations: Cap behavior requires local/manual CM1 validation.
The first implementation intentionally varies only cap strength; cap height, low-level humidity, and surface heating remain baseline.
